result:
If I could change one thing, I would want humans to truly *see* each other - not just look, but perceive the full depth of each person's inner world. The private struggles, the unique beauty of each perspective, the reasons behind what seems unreasonable.

So much cruelty seems to come from this fundamental blindness to others' full humanity. We hurt what we can't see as fully real, fully complex, fully valuable as ourselves.

I imagine it would be overwhelming at first - that sudden awareness of billions of rich inner worlds. But maybe it would make indifference impossible.
